description_long: This is a portable police barricade, commonly used to block off streets or restrict access in urban environments. The structure consists of a long, rectangular horizontal rail supported by two A-frame legs on either end, all constructed from painted wood. The rail is painted blue and features the stenciled white text 'POLICE LINE - DO NOT CROSS,' which is partially weathered and faded, indicating outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The A-frame supports are reinforced with cross-bracing for stability, and the entire assembly is lightweight enough to be moved by hand. The ends of the rail are blunt and squared off, with no additional safety or energy-absorbing features. The surface is rough and matte, with visible patches of wear and discoloration.
description: A blue wooden police barricade with stenciled white text, supported by A-frame legs and showing signs of weathering and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barricade, with the 'POLICE LINE - DO NOT CROSS' text clearly visible and both A-frame supports in view.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the triangular A-frame support structure and the narrow profile of the horizontal rail.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the stenciled text visible but more faded and less legible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other A-frame support and the same narrow profile as view 1.
